g.text: Mutex vs Spinlock: When to Use Which (Linux Kernel)
Hardware Soul: Spinlock on x86-64 SMP
OUR DRIVER: Why We MUST Use Mutex
3 | ZF=0: lock held — call __mutex_lock | slow path
4 | spin briefly (MCS queue, ~100 iters)| avoid immediate sleep
6 | schedule() — give up CPU | context switch ~1-10us
7 | Woken by mutex_unlock: CMPXCHG again | reacquire
2 | head == my_ticket? | check if our turn
4 | Yes: memory barrier (MFENCE)| ensure prior loads visible
6 | Critical section executes | OTHER CPUs spin here
7 | spin_unlock: LOCK INC [head]| wake next ticket holder
Cost: ~5ns acquire if uncontended | Contended: burns CPU cycles
Context | called via syscall → process context ✓
Sleeps? | kzalloc(GFP_KERNEL) can sleep for pages ✓
VERDICT | spinlock here = GUARANTEED DEADLOCK or OOPS
Usable in IRQ ctx | NO — will oops | YES (with irqsave)
Preemption | Allowed while wait | Disabled while held
mutex_lock inside | (no nesting w/out trylock) | FORBIDDEN
Hold time | Milliseconds OK | Microseconds ONLY
SMP contention cost | Schedule (~10us) | Burns all contending CPUs
UP (single CPU) | Works normally | Compiles to preempt_disable
Our driver verdict | USE THIS | DO NOT USE
Mutex use case | file_operations handlers, work queue callbacks
Mutex use case | ioctl with copy_to/from_user inside
Mutex use case | buffer resize (kzalloc + memcpy)
Mutex use case | any section lasting > a few microseconds
Spinlock use | IRQ handler updating a shared counter
Spinlock use | Protecting a list head accessed from softirq
Spinlock use | Per-CPU data where preemption must be off
Spinlock use | Time-critical sections measured in cycles
On a single CPU, no other CPU can enter the critical section
Busy-waiting is pointless — holder IS the only CPU
Spinlock compiles to zero instructions for the lock itself
Only preemption is disabled to prevent task switch
spin_lock_irqsave() still needed: interrupts can preempt
2. Process A: copy_to_user() → userspace page not in RAM
3. MMU raises page fault → kernel tries to swap page in
4. Swap requires I/O wait → tries to schedule()
5. CANNOT schedule: preemption is disabled by spinlock!
6. KERNEL OOPS: 'BUG: sleeping function called from invalid context'
7. On RT kernels: immediate panic. On !RT: undefined corruption.
